headline: TSMC Sales Grow 30% on Sustained Global Demand for AI Hardware
summary: The go-to chipmaker for Nvidia Corp., Advanced Micro Devices Inc. and Broadcom Inc. serves as a barometer for the global AI industry, with attention now on how the US and Israel’s strike on Iran will affect appetite to build data centers and other digital infrastructure.  The construction of AI data centers can cost tens of billions of dollars and require coordination with power generation and electrical grid operators, as well as suppliers and debt providers.  Oracle Corp. and OpenAI have scrapped plans to expand a flagship artificial intelligence data center in Texas after negotiations dragged over financing and OpenAI’s changing needs, Bloomberg News reported last week.
